frameworks.name: Bayesian observer
frameworks.one_line: post-choice evidence reweighting as suboptimal vs calibrated inference.

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: categorical clockwise/counterclockwise judgment relative to a reference.

core_question: Does incentivized feedback eliminate confirmation bias in post-choice perceptual evidence integration?
key_assumptions: observers integrate sequential samples to estimate a hidden mean
a categorical choice can bias weighting of subsequent evidence
monetary reward + ground-truth feedback can calibrate inference
manipulations: presence vs absence of trial-by-trial feedback with monetary reward
categorical choice after 4 vs all 8 samples
estimate timing: immediately post-choice vs after remaining samples
key_findings: incentivized feedback substantially reduced confirmation bias
confirmation bias was not eliminated by feedback
post-choice evidence weighting remained choice-congruent under reward
interpretations: prior confirmation biases partly reflect suboptimal strategy from missing feedback
residual bias reflects a deliberate process weighting objectives beyond accuracy
